section_title: 10 DETAILS OF THE UNDERLYING SCENARIOS USED TO CALCULATE THE IMPACTS

scenarios.title: A1 - raw material supply
scenarios.description: This module takes into account the extraction and processing of all raw materials and energy which occur upstream to the studied manufacturing process. These are materials of Orbix, water, glue, packaging materials, liquid CO2 and expanded clay pellets. An EPD of the expanded clay pellets is used, and primary data of the production process at Orbix has been used to model the materials.

scenarios.title: A2 – transport to the manufacturer
scenarios.description: The raw materials are transported to the manufacturing site.

scenarios.title: A3 – manufacturing
scenarios.description:
This module takes into account the production process.

At Betonagglomeraten Gubbels, Orbix raw materials and Argex expanded clay pellets are mixed and pressed as a mixture in a block form on a vibrating press. Next, this pressed mix goes to a climate chamber, here the liquid CO2 is injected into the process. It is measured how much CO2 enters the chamber and how much is absorbed by the stones through carbonation and calcium carbonate is created. After this, the stones are polished if necessary. Finally, they go to a stacking/packing machine, which sorts the Carbstones for storage and transport to customers.

The energy input in the manufacturing is electricity from photovoltaic panels.

scenarios.title: A4 – transport to the building site
scenarios.description:
Fuel type and consumption of vehicle or vehicle type used for transport: Truck 16-32 ton, 0,256 l diesel / km
Distance: 75 km
Capacity utilisation (including empty returns): 50%
Bulk density of transported products: Ecoinvent
Volume capacity utilisation factor: Ecoinvent

Betonagglomeraten Gubbels NV had reported that deliveries are made to several locations to distributors. The average distance to the middlemen is 40 km.

There is also flat rate that should be used in the absence of specific data, which is the case here. This distance from the distributor to the construction site is 35 km.

The following transport steps apply:
– 100% is transported over 40 km from supplier to distributors with a 16-32 ton lorry (ecoinvent record: 'Transport, freight, lorry 16-32 metric ton, EURO5 {RER}| transport, freight, lorry 16-32 metric ton, EURO5 | Cut-off, U')
– 100% is transported over 35 km from distributor to construction site with a 16-32 ton lorry (ecoinvent record: 'Transport, freight, lorry 16-32 metric ton, EURO5 {RER}| transport, freight, lorry 16-32 metric ton, EURO5 | Cut-off, U')

scenarios.title: A5 – installation in the building
scenarios.description:
At the construction site, packaging materials are released. Also 3% material losses have been taken into account of the Carbstone and 15% of the glue.

The Carbstone has a similar function to concrete, therefore it was also chosen to follow this waste scenario. This means 99% recycling and 1% landfill. This differs from the concrete waste scenario prescribed by B-EPD, the OVAM prescribes (based on Article 4.3.2 Vlarema) that construction rubble must be completely separated and recycled. As it is difficult in practice to recycle all construction rubble 100%, a conservative approach of 1% landfill has been assumed. Since the glue is not collected and processed separately, it follows the same waste scenario as the Carbstone.

Parts of the installation quantity Description
Material losses – Carbstone: 3% - THE QUANTITY OF MATERIAL LOST DUE TO CUTTING IT IN THE RIGHT SHAPE
Material losses – glue: 15% - THE QUANTITY OF MATERIAL LOST DUE TO CUTTING IT IN THE RIGHT SHAPE
Packaging – Wooden Pallet: 0,1 – 0,3 kg - THE PACKAGING WASTE AT THE CONSTRUCTION SITE
Packaging – PET straps: < 0,1 kg - THE PACKAGING WASTE AT THE CONSTRUCTION SITE

scenarios.title: B – use stage (excluding potential savings)
scenarios.description: In the use phase, carbonation also takes place over the years. This is because there are still CaOH present that will eventually react with CO2 from the ambient air, this is converted to calcium carbonates. The carbonation of the blocks in the use phase is 1,87%.

scenarios.title: C – end of life
scenarios.description:
The Carbstone has a similar function to concrete, therefore it was also chosen to follow this waste scenario. This means 99% recycling and 1% landfill. This differs from the concrete waste scenario prescribed by B-EPD, the OVAM prescribes (based on Article 4.3.2 Vlarema) that construction rubble must be completely separated and recycled. As it is difficult in practice to recycle all construction rubble 100%, a conservative approach of 1% landfill has been assumed.

The adhesive follows the scenario finishing layers stuck to stony waste. It is assumed that the glue follows the same scenario, they are also not separated so therefore follow the same processes.

Module C2 – Transport to waste processing
Recycling: Truck 16-32 ton, 0,256 l diesel/km, Distance 130 km, Capacity utilisation 50%, Density of products: Ecoinvent scenario, Assumptions: Ecoinvent scenario
Landfill: Truck 16-32 ton, 0,256 l diesel/km, Distance 80 km, Capacity utilisation 50%, Density of products: Ecoinvent scenario, Assumptions: Ecoinvent scenario

End-of-life modules – C3 and C4
Wastes collected separately: -
Wastes collected as mixed construction waste: -
Waste for re-use: -
Waste for recycling: 99%
Waste for energy recovery: -
Waste for final disposal: 1%

scenarios.title: D – benefits and loads beyond the system boundaries
scenarios.description:
QUANTITATIVE DESCRIPTION OF THE LOADS BEYOND THE SYSTEM BOUNDARIES: No loads

QUANTITATIVE DESCRIPTION OF THE BENEFITS BEYOND THE SYSTEM BOUNDARIES: Avoided production of stone granulates.
